001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect;
018
019import static java.util.stream.Collectors.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
024
025import java.lang.reflect.*;
026import java.util.*;
027
028import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
029import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
030
031/**
032 * Lightweight utility class for introspecting information about a Java constructor.
033 *
034 * <p>
035 * This class provides a convenient wrapper around {@link Constructor} that extends the standard Java reflection
036 * API with additional functionality for constructor introspection, annotation handling, and instance creation.
037 * It extends {@link ExecutableInfo} to provide common functionality shared with methods.
038 *
039 * <h5 class='section'>Features:</h5>
040 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
041 *    <li>Constructor introspection - access constructor metadata, parameters, exceptions
042 *    <li>Annotation support - get annotations declared on the constructor
043 *    <li>Instance creation - create new instances with type safety
044 *    <li>Accessibility control - make private constructors accessible
045 *    <li>Thread-safe - instances are immutable and safe for concurrent access
046 * </ul>
047 *
048 * <h5 class='section'>Use Cases:</h5>
049 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
050 *    <li>Introspecting constructor metadata for code generation or analysis
051 *    <li>Creating instances of classes dynamically
052 *    <li>Finding annotations on constructors
053 *    <li>Working with constructor parameters and exceptions
054 *    <li>Building frameworks that need to instantiate objects
055 * </ul>
056 *
057 * <h5 class='section'>Usage:</h5>
058 * <p class='bjava'>
059 *    <jc>// Get ConstructorInfo from a class</jc>
060 *    ClassInfo <jv>ci</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>);
061 *    ConstructorInfo <jv>ctor</jv> = <jv>ci</jv>.getConstructor(String.<jk>class</jk>);
062 *
063 *    <jc>// Get annotations</jc>
064 *    List&lt;AnnotationInfo&lt;MyAnnotation&gt;&gt; <jv>annotations</jv> =
065 *       <jv>ctor</jv>.getAnnotations(MyAnnotation.<jk>class</jk>).toList();
066 *
067 *    <jc>// Create instance</jc>
068 *    <jv>ctor</jv>.accessible();  <jc>// Make accessible if private</jc>
069 *    MyClass <jv>obj</jv> = <jv>ctor</jv>.invoke(<js>"arg"</js>);
070 * </p>
071 *
072 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
073 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ClassInfo} - Class introspection
074 *    <li class='jc'>{@link MethodInfo} - Method introspection
075 *    <li class='jc'>{@link FieldInfo} - Field introspection
076 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauCommonsReflection">Reflection Package</a>
077 * </ul>
078 */
079@SuppressWarnings({
080   "java:S115" // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
081})
082public final class ConstructorInfo extends ExecutableInfo implements Comparable<ConstructorInfo>, Annotatable {
083
084   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
085   private static final String ARG_declaringClass = "declaringClass";
086   private static final String ARG_inner = "inner";
087
088   /**
089    * Creates a ConstructorInfo wrapper for the specified constructor.
090    *
091    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
092    * <p class='bjava'>
093    *    ClassInfo <jv>ci</jv> = ClassInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyClass.<jk>class</jk>);
094    *    Constructor&lt;?&gt; <jv>c</jv> = MyClass.<jk>class</jk>.getConstructor(String.<jk>class</jk>);
095    *    ConstructorInfo <jv>ci2</jv> = ConstructorInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>ci</jv>, <jv>c</jv>);
096    * </p>
097    *
098    * @param declaringClass The ClassInfo for the class that declares this constructor. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
099    * @param inner The constructor being wrapped. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
100    * @return A new ConstructorInfo object wrapping the constructor.
101    */
102   public static ConstructorInfo of(ClassInfo declaringClass, Constructor<?> inner) {
103      assertArgNotNull(ARG_declaringClass, declaringClass);
104      return declaringClass.getConstructor(inner);
105   }
106
107   /**
108    * Creates a ConstructorInfo wrapper for the specified constructor.
109    *
110    * <p>
111    * This convenience method automatically determines the declaring class from the constructor.
112    *
113    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
114    * <p class='bjava'>
115    *    Constructor&lt;?&gt; <jv>c</jv> = MyClass.<jk>class</jk>.getConstructor(String.<jk>class</jk>);
116    *    ConstructorInfo <jv>ci</jv> = ConstructorInfo.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>c</jv>);
117    * </p>
118    *
119    * @param inner The constructor being wrapped. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
120    * @return A new ConstructorInfo object wrapping the constructor.
121    */
122   public static ConstructorInfo of(Constructor<?> inner) {
123      assertArgNotNull(ARG_inner, inner);
124      return ClassInfo.of(inner.getDeclaringClass()).getConstructor(inner);
125   }
126
127   private final Constructor<?> inner;
128
129   /**
130    * Constructor.
131    *
132    * <p>
133    * Creates a new ConstructorInfo wrapper for the specified constructor. This constructor is protected
134    * and should not be called directly. Use the static factory methods {@link #of(Constructor)} or
135    * obtain ConstructorInfo instances from {@link ClassInfo#getConstructor(Constructor)}.
136    *
137    * @param declaringClass The ClassInfo for the class that declares this constructor.
138    * @param inner The constructor being wrapped.
139    */
140   protected ConstructorInfo(ClassInfo declaringClass, Constructor<?> inner) {
141      super(declaringClass, inner);
142      this.inner = inner;
143   }
144
145   @Override /* Overridden from ExecutableInfo */
146   public ConstructorInfo accessible() {
147      super.accessible();
148      return this;
149   }
150
151   @Override
152   public int compareTo(ConstructorInfo o) {
153      int i = cmp(getNameSimple(), o.getNameSimple());
154      if (i == 0) {
155         i = getParameterCount() - o.getParameterCount();
156         if (i == 0) {
157            var params = getParameters();
158            var oParams = o.getParameters();
159            for (var j = 0; j < params.size() && i == 0; j++) {
160               i = cmp(params.get(j).getParameterType().getName(), oParams.get(j).getParameterType().getName());
161            }
162         }
163      }
164      return i;
165   }
166
167   @Override /* Annotatable */
168   public AnnotatableType getAnnotatableType() { return AnnotatableType.CONSTRUCTOR_TYPE; }
169
170   @Override /* Annotatable */
171   public String getLabel() { return getDeclaringClass().getNameSimple() + "." + getNameShort(); }
172
173   /**
174    * Returns the wrapped constructor.
175    *
176    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
177    * <p class='bjava'>
178    *    ConstructorInfo <jv>ci</jv> = ...;
179    *    Constructor&lt;MyClass&gt; <jv>ctor</jv> = <jv>ci</jv>.inner();
180    * </p>
181    *
182    * @param <T> The class type of the constructor.
183    * @return The wrapped constructor.
184    */
185   @SuppressWarnings({
186      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast to Constructor<T>
187   })
188   public <T> Constructor<T> inner() {
189      return (Constructor<T>)inner;
190   }
191
192   /**
193    * Compares this ConstructorInfo with the specified object for equality.
194    *
195    * <p>
196    * Two ConstructorInfo objects are considered equal if they wrap the same underlying {@link Constructor} object.
197    * This delegates to the underlying {@link Constructor#equals(Object)} method.
198    *
199    * <p>
200 * This method makes ConstructorInfo suitable for use as keys in hash-based collections such as {@link HashMap}
201 * and {@link HashSet}.
202    *
203    * @param obj The object to compare with.
204    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the objects are equal, <jk>false</jk> otherwise.
205    */
206   @Override
207   public boolean equals(Object obj) {
208      return obj instanceof ConstructorInfo other && eq(this, other, (x, y) -> eq(x.inner, y.inner));
209   }
210
211   /**
212    * Returns a hash code value for this ConstructorInfo.
213    *
214    * <p>
215    * This delegates to the underlying {@link Constructor#hashCode()} method.
216    *
217    * <p>
218 * This method makes ConstructorInfo suitable for use as keys in hash-based collections such as {@link HashMap}
219 * and {@link HashSet}.
220    *
221    * @return A hash code value for this ConstructorInfo.
222    */
223   @Override
224   public int hashCode() {
225      return inner.hashCode();
226   }
227
228   /**
229    * Shortcut for calling the new-instance method on the underlying constructor.
230    *
231    * <p>
232    * If the number of formal parameters required by the underlying constructor is 0, the supplied <c>args</c> array may be of length 0 or <jk>null</jk>.
233    *
234    * @param <T> The constructor class type.
235    * @param args The arguments used for the constructor call.  Can be <jk>null</jk> or empty for constructors with no parameters.
236    * @return The object returned from the constructor.
237    * @throws ExecutableException Exception occurred on invoked constructor/method/field.
238    */
239   @SuppressWarnings({
240      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast to T for instance creation
241   })
242   public <T> T newInstance(Object...args) throws ExecutableException {
243      return safe(() -> {
244         try {
245            return (T)inner.newInstance(args);
246         } catch (InvocationTargetException e) {
247            throw exex(e.getTargetException());
248         }
249      }, e -> exex(e));  // HTT
250   }
251
252   /**
253    * Shortcut for calling the new-instance method on the underlying constructor using lenient argument matching.
254    *
255    * <p>
256    * Lenient matching allows arguments to be matched to parameters based on parameter types.
257    * <br>Arguments can be in any order.
258    * <br>Extra arguments are ignored.
259    * <br>Missing arguments are set to <jk>null</jk>.
260    *
261    * @param <T> The constructor class type.
262    * @param args The arguments used for the constructor call.
263    * @return The object returned from the constructor.
264    * @throws ExecutableException Exception occurred on invoked constructor/method/field.
265    */
266   public <T> T newInstanceLenient(Object...args) throws ExecutableException {
267      return newInstance(ClassUtils.getMatchingArgs(inner.getParameterTypes(), args));
268   }
269
270   /**
271    * Returns comma-delimited list of missing parameter types.
272    *
273    * <p>
274    * Analyzes the parameters of this constructor and checks if all required beans are available
275    * in the bean store or in the <c>otherBeans</c> parameter.
276    *
277    * <p>
278    * The following parameter types are considered optional and are not checked:
279    * <ul>
280    *    <li>Parameters of type <c>Optional&lt;T&gt;</c>
281    *    <li>Parameters of type <c>T[]</c>, <c>List&lt;T&gt;</c>, <c>Set&lt;T&gt;</c>, or <c>Map&lt;String,T&gt;</c>
282    *    <li>The first parameter if it matches the <c>enclosingInstance</c> object type (for non-static inner classes)
283    * </ul>
284    *
285    * <p>
286    * If a parameter has a <ja>@Named</ja> or {@code @Qualifier} annotation,
287    * the method checks for a bean with that specific name in the bean store.  Otherwise, it checks for an unnamed bean
288    * of the parameter type in the bean store, and if not found, checks the <c>otherBeans</c> parameter.
289    *
290    * @param beanStore The bean store to check for beans.
291    * @param enclosingInstance The outer class instance for non-static inner class constructors.
292    *    If the first parameter type matches this object's type, it is used as the first parameter and not checked in the bean store.
293    *    Can be <jk>null</jk> for regular classes or static inner classes.
294    * @param otherBeans Optional additional bean instances to check if not found in the bean store.
295    *    These are checked after the bean store but before marking the parameter as missing.
296    * @return A comma-delimited, sorted list of missing parameter types (e.g., <js>"String,Integer"</js>),
297    *    or <jk>null</jk> if all required parameters are available.
298    */
299   public String getMissingParameterTypes(BeanStore beanStore, Object enclosingInstance, Object... otherBeans) {
300      // @formatter:off
301      return nullIfEmpty(
302         getParameters()
303            .stream()
304            .map(x -> {
305               var pt = x.getParameterType();
306               if (x.getIndex() == 0 && nn(enclosingInstance) && pt.isInstance(enclosingInstance))
307                  return null;
308               return x.getMissingType(beanStore, otherBeans);
309            })
310            .filter(Objects::nonNull)
311            .sorted()
312            .collect(joining(","))
313      );
314      // @formatter:on
315   }
316
317   /**
318    * Checks if all constructor parameters can be resolved.
319    *
320    * <p>
321    * This method performs the same checks as {@link #getMissingParameterTypes(BeanStore, Object, Object...)} but
322    * returns a boolean instead of a list of missing types.
323    *
324    * <p>
325    * The following parameter types are considered optional and are not checked:
326    * <ul>
327    *    <li>Parameters of type <c>Optional&lt;T&gt;</c>
328    *    <li>Parameters of type <c>T[]</c>, <c>List&lt;T&gt;</c>, <c>Set&lt;T&gt;</c>, or <c>Map&lt;String,T&gt;</c>
329    *    <li>The first parameter if it matches the <c>enclosingInstance</c> object type (for non-static inner classes)
330    * </ul>
331    *
332    * <p>
333    * If a parameter has a <ja>@Named</ja> or {@code @Qualifier} annotation,
334    * the method checks for a bean with that specific name in the bean store.  Otherwise, it checks for an unnamed bean
335    * of the parameter type in the bean store, and if not found, checks the <c>otherBeans</c> parameter.
336    *
337    * @param beanStore The bean store to check for beans.
338    * @param enclosingInstance The outer class instance for non-static inner class constructors.
339    *    If the first parameter type matches this object's type, it is used as the first parameter and not checked in the bean store.
340    *    Can be <jk>null</jk> for regular classes or static inner classes.
341    * @param otherBeans Optional additional bean instances to check if not found in the bean store.
342    *    These are checked after the bean store but before marking the parameter as missing.
343    * @return <jk>true</jk> if all required parameters are available in the bean store or <c>otherBeans</c>, <jk>false</jk> otherwise.
344    */
345   public boolean canResolveAllParameters(BeanStore beanStore, Object enclosingInstance, Object... otherBeans) {
346      return getParameters().stream().map(x -> {
347         var pt = x.getParameterType();
348         if (x.getIndex() == 0 && nn(enclosingInstance) && pt.isInstance(enclosingInstance))
349            return true;
350         return x.canResolve(beanStore, otherBeans);
351      }).allMatch(x -> x);
352   }
353
354   /**
355    * Resolves parameters from the bean store and invokes the constructor.
356    *
357    * <p>
358    * For each parameter in the constructor, this method:
359    * <ul>
360    *    <li>If the first parameter type matches the <c>enclosingInstance</c> object type, uses the <c>enclosingInstance</c> object
361    *       (for non-static inner class constructors).
362    *    <li>If the parameter is a collection/array/map type, collects all beans of the element type.
363    *    <li>Otherwise, looks up a single bean by type and optional qualifier name in the bean store.
364    *    <li>If not found in the bean store, checks the <c>otherBeans</c> parameter.
365    * </ul>
366    *
367    * <h5 class='section'>Parameter Resolution:</h5>
368    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
369    *    <li><b>Single beans</b> - Resolved using {@link BeanStore#getBean(Class)} or {@link BeanStore#getBean(Class, String)}.
370    *       If not found, checks <c>otherBeans</c> for a compatible instance.
371    *       Throws {@link ExecutableException} if not found in either location.
372    *    <li><b>Optional beans</b> - Wrapped in <c>Optional</c>, or <c>Optional.empty()</c> if not found.
373    *       Never throws an exception.
374    *    <li><b>Arrays</b> - All beans of the element type are collected into an array (may be empty).
375    *       Never throws an exception.
376    *    <li><b>Lists</b> - All beans of the element type are collected into a <c>List</c> (may be empty).
377    *       Never throws an exception.
378    *    <li><b>Sets</b> - All beans of the element type are collected into a <c>LinkedHashSet</c> (may be empty).
379    *       Never throws an exception.
380    *    <li><b>Maps</b> - All beans of the value type are collected into a <c>LinkedHashMap</c> keyed by bean name (may be empty).
381    *       Unnamed beans use an empty string as the key.  Never throws an exception.
382    * </ul>
383    *
384    * <p>
385    * The <c>enclosingInstance</c> parameter is used for non-static inner class constructors.  If the first parameter type
386    * matches this object's type, it is used as the first parameter value.  For regular constructors, this parameter
387    * is ignored if it doesn't match the first parameter type.
388    *
389    * @param <T> The return type of the constructor.
390    * @param beanStore The bean store to resolve parameters from.
391    * @param enclosingInstance The outer class instance for non-static inner classes (can be <jk>null</jk>).
392    * @param otherBeans Optional additional bean instances to use if not found in the bean store.
393    * @return The result of invoking the constructor.
394    * @throws ExecutableException If the constructor cannot be invoked or parameter resolution fails.
395    */
396   public <T> T inject(BeanStore beanStore, Object enclosingInstance, Object... otherBeans) {
397      var params = getParameters().stream().map(x -> x.resolveValue(beanStore, enclosingInstance, otherBeans)).toArray();
398      return accessible().newInstance(params);
399   }
400
401   /**
402    * Resolves all constructor parameters from the bean store.
403    *
404    * <p>
405    * This is a package-private helper method used by tests.  For normal usage, use {@link #inject(BeanStore, Object, Object...)}.
406    *
407    * @param beanStore The bean store to resolve beans from.
408    * @param enclosingInstance The outer class instance for non-static inner class constructors.
409    * @param otherBeans Optional additional bean instances to use if not found in the bean store.
410    * @return An array of parameter values in the same order as the constructor parameters.
411    * @throws ExecutableException If a required parameter cannot be resolved.
412    */
413   Object[] resolveParameters(BeanStore beanStore, Object enclosingInstance, Object... otherBeans) {
414      return getParameters().stream().map(x -> x.resolveValue(beanStore, enclosingInstance, otherBeans)).toArray();
415   }
416}
